title: quercus × fernaldii
intro: Quercus × fernaldii is a hybrid oak tree species, resulting from the cross between Quercus alba (white oak) and Quercus rubra (red oak). The plant contains flavonoids, tannins, and other bioactive compounds, which have been found to possess medicinal properties, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Quercus × fernaldii are attributed to its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit immunomodulatory effects. Quercus × fernaldii is used in herbal preparations, such as tea infusions, tinctures, and capsules, to promote overall well-being and prevent various chronic diseases.
